title: quercus chrysolepis var. tomentella
intro: Quercus chrysolepis var. tomentella, also known as Island Live Oak, is a variant of the Island Buckeye tree, a type of evergreen oak native to the coastal regions of California and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Quercus chrysolepis var. tomentella include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and bark can be used in herbal preparations, such as salves and poultices, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
